county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Merrick County formed 1858 from Hall County. County seat is Central City. Platte Valley county. Union Pacific mainline. Settled in the late 1850s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Merrick County (formed 1858, seat Central City) records are at the courthouse in Central City. Merrick County lies in the Platte Valley and was settled in the late 1850s-1860s. The Union Pacific Railroad mainline runs through the county. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German, Irish, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. The Merrick County Historical Society may have local materials. Central City newspapers from the 1870s are useful sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Merrick County. County Register of Deeds in Central City holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Merrick County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Merrick County Register of Deeds, Central City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Merrick County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1858. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Merrick County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1858. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Merrick County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Merrick County (1860-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Merrick County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Merrick County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Merrick County history.

source_profiles.repository: Union Pacific Historical Collection / History Nebraska / Nebraska State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Union Pacific Railroad land grant and sale records for Merrick County. The UP received alternate sections of land along its right-of-way. Land department records document sales to settlers, town lot sales, and colony arrangements. UP Historical Collection at History Nebraska.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Morrill County formed 1908 from Cheyenne County. County seat is Bridgeport. Panhandle county. Courthouse and Jail Rocks (Oregon Trail landmarks). Irrigation and sugar beet farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Morrill County (formed 1908, seat Bridgeport) is one of Nebraska's youngest counties, carved from Cheyenne County. Records are at the courthouse in Bridgeport. Courthouse and Jail Rocks (Oregon Trail landmarks) are in Morrill County. Settlement was driven by irrigation projects and homesteading. BLM GLO records document land entries. Sugar beet farming attracted Hispanic/Latino workers. German-Russian settlers were significant. Pre-1908 records are in Cheyenne County. FamilySearch has limited coverage. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Bridgeport newspapers are useful local sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Morrill County. County Register of Deeds in Bridgeport holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

History Nebraska holds manuscript collections. Most trail traffic passed through before county organization.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nance County formed 1879 from the dissolved Pawnee Indian Reservation. County seat is Fullerton. Central Nebraska. Created when Pawnee were removed to Indian Territory (Oklahoma).
county_metadata.research_tips: Nance County (formed 1879, seat Fullerton) is unique because it was created from the dissolved Pawnee Indian Reservation. Records are at the courthouse in Fullerton. The Pawnee tribe was removed to Indian Territory (Oklahoma) in the 1870s -- researchers with Pawnee connections should check NARA Kansas City for Pawnee Agency records, tribal census rolls, and allotment records. After the reservation was dissolved, the land was opened to homesteading. BLM GLO records document entries. German, Czech, and Anglo-American settlers acquired the former reservation lands. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds Pawnee-related materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Nance County. County Register of Deeds in Fullerton holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nemaha County formed 1854 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Auburn. Southeastern Nebraska. Brownville was an important Missouri River town. Peru State College (1867).
county_metadata.research_tips: Nemaha County (formed 1854, seat Auburn) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties in the southeastern corner. Records are at the courthouse in Auburn. Being an original county, Nemaha has some of the earliest records in the state, dating to 1854. Brownville, on the Missouri River, was the territorial capital's rival and an important river town -- the Brownville Historical Society preserves materials. Peru State College (1867) was Nebraska's first college. Homestead and pre-emption entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German, Irish, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds territorial-era records. Nemaha County newspapers from the 1850s-1860s are among the earliest in Nebraska.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Nemaha County. County Register of Deeds in Auburn holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nuckolls County formed 1860 from unorganized territory. County seat is Nelson. South-central Nebraska along the Republican River. Superior on the Kansas border.
county_metadata.research_tips: Nuckolls County (formed 1860, seat Nelson) in south-central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Nelson. The Republican River runs through the county. Settlement came through homesteading in the 1870s-1880s. BLM GLO records document homestead entries. Superior, on the Kansas border, is the largest community. German, Czech, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Superior newspapers from the 1870s-1880s provide genealogical information.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Nuckolls County. County Register of Deeds in Nelson holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Otoe County formed 1854 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Nebraska City. Missouri River steamboat landing and freighting center. J. Sterling Morton (Arbor Day). Mayhew Cabin (Underground Railroad).
county_metadata.research_tips: Otoe County (formed 1854, seat Nebraska City) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties along the Missouri River. Records are at the courthouse in Nebraska City. Nebraska City was a major Missouri River steamboat landing and freighting center -- J. Sterling Morton (founder of Arbor Day) lived here. The Otoe County Museum of Memories has local collections. Mayhew Cabin (Underground Railroad site) in Nebraska City is significant for African American history. Being an original county, Otoe has territorial-era records from 1854. Homestead and pre-emption entries at BLM GLO document settlers. German, Irish, and Anglo-American families predominated. FamilySearch has extensive microfilm. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds territorial-era records. Nebraska City newspapers from the 1850s are among the state's earliest.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Otoe County. County Register of Deeds in Nebraska City holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pawnee County formed 1855 from Gage and Richardson counties. County seat is Pawnee City. Southeastern Nebraska. Named for the Pawnee tribe. Borders Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pawnee County (formed 1855, seat Pawnee City) records are at the courthouse in Pawnee City. Named for the Pawnee tribe, this southeastern Nebraska county was among the earliest organized. The county borders Kansas. Homestead and pre-emption entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German, Anglo-American, and Swedish settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Table Rock and DuBois were other significant communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Pawnee County. County Register of Deeds in Pawnee City holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Perkins County formed 1887 from Keith County. County seat is Grant. Southwestern Nebraska. Burlington Railroad settlement. Ranching and dry-land farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Perkins County (formed 1887, seat Grant) in southwestern Nebraska was carved from Keith County. Records are at the courthouse in Grant (the town, not Grant County). Settlement was driven by homesteading in the late 1880s-1890s. BLM GLO records document homestead entries. The Burlington Railroad brought settlers. Cattle ranching and dry-land farming are the economic base. Anglo-American and German settlers predominated. FamilySearch has limited microfilm. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Perkins County. County Register of Deeds in Grant holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Perkins County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Perkins County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

source_profiles.source_name: Perkins County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / Historical Society
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Perkins County Historical Society / Nebraska State Genealogical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions and burial records for Perkins County. Find A Grave has growing coverage. Township, church, and family cemeteries are common in rural Nebraska. County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

source_profiles.source_name: Perkins County Records on Microfilm -- History Nebraska
source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Perkins County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Perkins County history.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Phelps County formed 1873 from unorganized territory. County seat is Holdrege. South-central Nebraska. Strong Swedish settlement. Burlington Railroad division point.
county_metadata.research_tips: Phelps County (formed 1873, seat Holdrege) in south-central Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Holdrege. The Nebraska Prairie Museum in Holdrege has regional collections. Strong Swedish settlement in the Holdrege area -- Swedish Lutheran church records are important. The Burlington Railroad brought settlers. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Holdrege newspapers from the 1880s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Phelps County. County Register of Deeds in Holdrege holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Phelps County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Phelps County Register of Deeds, Holdrege
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1873 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Phelps County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1873. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Phelps County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1873. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Phelps County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Phelps County (1880-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

source_profiles.source_name: Phelps County Swedish Lutheran Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Augustana Synod Archives / Bethphage Mission / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Swedish Lutheran (Augustana Synod) church records for Phelps County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Records often in Swedish. FamilySearch has microfilmed some collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pierce County formed 1856 from unorganized territory. County seat is Pierce. Northeast Nebraska. Strong German settlement. Elkhorn River area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pierce County (formed 1856, seat Pierce) in northeast Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in Pierce. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. German settlers were particularly numerous in Pierce County. Lutheran and Catholic church records are important supplementary sources. The Elkhorn River influences the county's geography. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Plainview and Osmond were other significant communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Pierce County. County Register of Deeds in Pierce holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Pierce County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Pierce County Register of Deeds, Pierce
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Pierce County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1856. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Pierce County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1856. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Pierce County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Pierce County (1860-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.

source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Lincoln / Diocese of Grand Island / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Lutheran and Catholic church records for Pierce County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Records often in German. German-Russian (Volga German) congregations may have separate records.
